name: Nebraska School Bus Inc
districts_or_customers: Battle Creek area (NE), per address; districts not confirmed
recent_news: No M&A activity found. Note: a different NE operator, Mid States School Bus (Wayne, NE), was acquired by First Student in March 2021 — that is NOT this company. Nebraska School Bus Inc (Battle Creek) appears separate but its ownership and operating status remain unverified online.
fit_notes: Small NE operator (~40 employees, est. ~$1.8M revenue) in Summit footprint state. Low confidence on independence and ownership; warrants direct outreach to verify operator status, fleet, and contracts before tuck-in consideration.

name: Bluff Country School Bus Svc
recent_news: 2022: Doug Grisim publicly discussed driver-shortage hiring push for new school year (KTTC). Third-generation Grisim family operator; Doug is past President and current board member of Minnesota School Bus Operators Association.
fit_notes: Strong tuck-in candidate: independent, third-generation family operator, sole provider to 3 SE MN districts, ~50 buses. Founders active 30+ years with two adult children in business — medium succession signal (could go either way). MN is a Summit footprint state.

name: Kiessling School Trnsprtn
owner_or_parent: Mobico Group (formerly National Express) — same parent as Summit/Durham; operates under WeDriveU brand family
districts_or_customers: Massachusetts transit authorities, ADH centers, hospitals, child daycare/pre-schools, special education programs, colleges/universities; previously had Framingham, MA operations (laid off 124 in mid-2025)
recent_news: Acquired Dec 14, 2018 by National Express (now Mobico Group); operates under WeDriveU brand family alongside Cook DuPage Transportation, Diamond Transportation, Silver Fox Charters, Total Transit, White Plains Bus Company. 2025 layoffs of 124 employees in Framingham, MA reported.
fit_notes: NOT A TARGET — already owned by Mobico Group, the same parent that previously owned Summit/Durham. Sister-company within the platform's former parent family. Exclude from buyer list.

name: Monarch Bus Svc
owner_or_parent: Affiliated with Minnesota Coaches family of companies (Minnesota Coaches Inc, Minneapolis & Suburban Bus Co, Monarch Bus Service — treated as joint/single employers per NLRB case 18-CA-169411). Locally/family owned; Bruce Dischinger cited as owner of Monarch in third-party profile.
districts_or_customers: Twin Cities charter and private schools
recent_news: NLRB case 18-CA-169411 named Minnesota Coaches, Minneapolis & Suburban Bus, and Monarch Bus Service as joint/single employers. Minnesota Coaches family operates 11 locations across MN and WI; described as largest motorcoach operator in region. No 2024-2026 M&A found.
fit_notes: Likely tuck-in candidate as part of Minnesota Coaches family (65+ year family-owned group, 11 locations MN/WI). Holds key St Paul ISD 625 contract. Acquisition would likely require dealing at the Minnesota Coaches parent level, not Monarch standalone. MN is a Summit footprint state.

name: Rainbow Transportation Svc Inc
districts_or_customers: Windsor, CT area school transportation (specific district contracts not publicly confirmed)
recent_news: Long-standing CT operator (60+ years). Operates under two related entities: Rainbow Bus Lines Inc (CT entity 0038564) and Rainbow Transportation Services Inc (CT entity 0563293), both Viets-controlled. No M&A activity found in 2023-2026.
fit_notes: Solid tuck-in candidate: 60+ year independent Viets-family operator in CT (Summit footprint). Long tenure suggests founder/owner aging — medium succession signal. ~163 employees and est. ~$8.6M revenue is a meaningful regional bolt-on in the dense Hartford-area market.
